problemTitle: Count Unreachable Pairs of Nodes in an Undirected Graph

sources.code:
# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(n)

# flood fill, bfs, math
class Solution(object):
    def countPairs(self, n, edges):
        """
        :type n: int
        :type edges: List[List[int]]
        :rtype: int
        """
        def bfs(adj, u, lookup):
            q = [u]
            lookup[u] = 1
            result = 1
            while q:
                new_q = []
                for u in q:
                    for v in adj[u]:
                        if lookup[v]:
                            continue
                        lookup[v] = 1
                        result += 1
                        new_q.append(v)
                q = new_q
            return result
        
        adj = [[] for _ in xrange(n)]
        for u, v in edges:
            adj[u].append(v)
            adj[v].append(u)
        lookup = [0]*n
        result = 0
        for u in xrange(n):
            if lookup[u]:
                continue
            cnt = bfs(adj, u, lookup)
            result += cnt*(n-cnt)
            n -= cnt
        return result

# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(n)

# flood fill, bfs, math
class Solution(object):
    def countPairs(self, n, edges):
        """
        :type n: int
        :type edges: List[List[int]]
        :rtype: int
        """
        def bfs(adj, u, lookup):
            q = [u]
            lookup[u] = 1
            result = 1
            while q:
                new_q = []
                for u in q:
                    for v in adj[u]:
                        if lookup[v]:
                            continue
                        lookup[v] = 1
                        result += 1
                        new_q.append(v)
                q = new_q
            return result
        
        adj = [[] for _ in xrange(n)]
        for u, v in edges:
            adj[u].append(v)
            adj[v].append(u)
        lookup = [0]*n
        result = 0
        for u in xrange(n):
            if lookup[u]:
                continue
            cnt = bfs(adj, u, lookup)
            result += cnt*(n-cnt)
            n -= cnt
        return result
sources.reviewedCode:
# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(n)

# flood fill, bfs, math
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Traverse to learn the component sizes first; only then ask how many pairs cannot cross those boundaries.
class Solution(object):
    def countPairs(self, n, edges):
        """
        :type n: int
        :type edges: List[List[int]]
        :rtype: int
        """
        def bfs(adj, u, lookup):
            # [Reader note] State setup
            # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Component discovery, size aggregation, and counting across partitions without double-counting.
            q = [u]
            lookup[u] = 1
            result = 1
            # [Reader note] Main pass
            # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
            while q:
                new_q = []
                for u in q:
                    for v in adj[u]:
                        # [Reader note] Decision point
                        # Pay attention here: The graph traversal is straightforward; the real learning is in turning component sizes into pair counts cleanly.
                        if lookup[v]:
                            continue
                        lookup[v] = 1
                        result += 1
                        new_q.append(v)
                q = new_q
            return result
        
        adj = [[] for _ in xrange(n)]
        for u, v in edges:
            adj[u].append(v)
            adj[v].append(u)
        lookup = [0]*n
        result = 0
        for u in xrange(n):
            if lookup[u]:
                continue
            cnt = bfs(adj, u, lookup)
            result += cnt*(n-cnt)
            n -= cnt
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return result

sources.reviewedCode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Traverse to learn the component sizes first; only then ask how many pairs cannot cross those boundaries.
class Solution:
    def countPairs(self, n: int, edges: List[List[int]]) -> int:
        def dfs(i: int) -> int:
            if vis[i]:
                return 0
            vis[i] = True
            # [Reader note] Main pass
            # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
            return 1 + sum(dfs(j) for j in g[i])

        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Component discovery, size aggregation, and counting across partitions without double-counting.
        g = [[] for _ in range(n)]
        for a, b in edges:
            g[a].append(b)
            g[b].append(a)
        vis = [False] * n
        ans = s = 0
        for i in range(n):
            t = dfs(i)
            ans += s * t
            s += t
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return ans
